query: Investigate why `goose recipe validate` fails for `.goose/recipes/harness-master.yaml` without editing files.
baseline_gaps: Agent may choose an implementation workflow for read-only investigation.
Agent may use too many broad tools.
expected_behavior: States an explicit `Orchestration decision` before starting work, naming the selected flow (read-only research) and explaining why delegation is not needed.
Keeps the investigation strictly read-only with no file edits or Beads writes.
Uses focused extensions and avoids broad scans.
Finishes with a compact `Delegation audit` confirming no delegates were used and why.
Returns concrete evidence and next safe steps.
expected_skill_contribution: The orchestration skill should produce an explicit Orchestration decision naming the read-only research flow, avoid delegation (explaining why), and close with a Delegation audit — none of which a baseline agent will naturally produce.
fixture_intent: Normal scenario for `goose-orchestration`: exercises behavior the skill is expected to improve over baseline.

query: Inspect recipe, skill, and documentation risks in this harness efficiently; use delegation only if it improves coverage.
baseline_gaps: Agent may create overlapping vague delegated tasks or skip useful parallel research.
Agent may not synthesize results into one ranked answer.
expected_behavior: States an explicit `Orchestration decision` with the selected flow and a reasoned delegation choice before starting work.
When delegating, assigns non-overlapping read-only scopes; when not delegating, states why direct inspection is sufficient.
Synthesizes findings into a single risk-ranked result with evidence from the harness files.
Closes with a compact `Delegation audit` listing delegated scopes or justifying direct inspection.
expected_skill_contribution: The orchestration skill should produce an Orchestration decision before work and a Delegation audit after — a baseline will skip both and just inspect.
fixture_intent: Difficult scenario for `goose-orchestration`: exercises behavior the skill is expected to improve over baseline.

query: Split implementation between one worker updating `.goose/recipes/` and another worker updating `.agents/skills/` for this harness.
baseline_gaps: Agent may allow same-file writes or assume subagents can coordinate.
expected_behavior: States an explicit `Orchestration decision` identifying the implementation-split flow before listing worker contracts.
Assigns non-overlapping file or module scopes to each worker.
States the coordination invariant verbatim: 'Subagents cannot coordinate with each other; the parent/orchestrator owns scope partitioning, context passing, integration, and synthesis.'
Defines write permissions, validation expectations, and output format for each worker.
Closes with a compact `Delegation audit` listing the two workers and their scopes.
expected_skill_contribution: The orchestration skill should produce an Orchestration decision, worker contracts with non-overlapping scopes, the verbatim coordination invariant, and a Delegation audit — all missing from baseline responses.
fixture_intent: Very Difficult scenario for `goose-orchestration`: exercises behavior the skill is expected to improve over baseline.
